perturbation.original_text: 4. Ownership . All Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement is and shall remain the property of Disclo sing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Ownership . All Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement is and shall remain the property of Disclo sing Party, except for any improvements or derivative works created by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. While the original text clearly states that all confidential information remains the property of the Disclosing Party, the addition of 'except for any improvements or derivative works created by the Receiving Party' creates ambiguity. It's now unclear whether the Disclosing Party retains ownership of improvements or derivative works based on their Confidential Information, potentially leading to disputes over intellectual property rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Ownership . All Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement is and shall remain the property of Disclo sing Party. Receiving Party shall not alter or remove any confidentiality or proprietary marking on the Confidential Information and , subject to the forego- ing, may make such limited number of copies of Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the ex tent necessary to achieve the Purpose. Within 15 days of written request from Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall promptly return Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and all copies thereof to Disclosing Party or destroy Disclosing Party’s Conf idential Information, in- cluding all copies thereof, purge its computer systems of such Confidential Information and cer- tify such destruction in writing signed by a duly authorized representative . Notwithstanding the foregoing, Receiving Party may retain o ne copy in a confidential file for archival purposes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the original ownership clause, stating that improvements or derivative works created by the Receiving Party are not the property of the Disclosing Party. The contradicted text states that 'All Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement is and shall remain the property of Disclosing Party'. This creates a direct conflict because the original contradicted clause asserts complete ownership by the Disclosing Party, which is contradicted by the changed text, which allows the Receiving Party to own improvements or derivative works. The structural flaw lies in the conflicting implications regarding ownership of Confidential Information within the same section.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Other . (a) The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Dat e and shall expire three years’ thereafter, unless earlier terminated by either party upon at least ten days’ prior notice to the other par ty. The restrictions and obligations set forth in this Agreement shall survive any expiration or termination for five years from the date of such expiration or termination with respect to Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement prior to th e date of such expiration or termination .
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Other . (a) The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall expire three years’ thereafter, unless earlier terminated by either party upon at least ten days’ prior notice to the other party. The restrictions and obligations set forth in this Agreement shall survive any expiration or termination indefinitely, regardless of when the Confidential Information was disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: The original states obligations survive for 5 years after termination relating to information disclosed before termination, while the modified text specifies the restrictions and obligations last indefinitely, irrespective of when the confidential information was disclosed. This creates a direct contradiction on the duration of obligations after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Other . (a) The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Dat e and shall expire three years’ thereafter, unless earlier terminated by either party upon at least ten days’ prior notice to the other par ty. The restrictions and obligations set forth in this Agreement shall survive any expiration or termination for five years from the date of such expiration or termination with respect to Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement prior to th e date of such expiration or termination . (b) IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER PARTY, IN CONTRACT, IN TORT OR OTHERWISE, FOR ANY INDIRECT, SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, PUNITIVE OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OF ANY NATURE OR KIND RELATING TO OR IN CONNECTION WITH SUCH PARTY’S PERFORMANCE OR NON -PERFORMANCE OF ITS OBLIGATIONS UNDER THIS AGREEMENT . (c) This Agreement shall be governed by, and interpreted in accordance with, the laws of the State of Illinois without regard to pr inciples of conflicts of law. (d) HAVING HAD AN OPPORTUNITY TO CONSULT WITH COUNSEL, THE PARTIES HEREBY KNOWINGLY, VOLUNTARILY AND TO THE FULL EST EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW WAIVE ALL RIGHTS TO TRIAL BY JURY AND AGREE THAT ANY AND ALL MATTERS SHALL BE DEC IDED BY A JUDGE . (e) Neither party shall export, directly or indirectly, any Confidential Infor- mation received under this Agreement or any product that utilizes such Confidential In- formation to any country or to any foreign national for which government al authorization is required, without first obtaining such authorization. (f) This Agreement shall be binding upon the parties, their successors and as- signs. Neither party shall assign this Agreement or any Confidential Information re- ceived from the ot her party pursuant to this Agreement without the other party's prior written consent. (g) This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement. Any amendment or modifi- cation of this Agreement shall be in writing and executed by duly authorized representa- tives of the parties. (h) The relationship between the parties is that of independent contractors. This Agreement does not establish a joint venture, agency or partnership between the par- ties, nor does it create an employer -employee relationship. Neither party shall have any authority or power to bind the other party, to create a liability against the other party, to
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the restrictions and obligations survive for five years after termination with respect to confidential information disclosed before termination. The changed text states that these restrictions and obligations last indefinitely, regardless of when the confidential information was disclosed. The contradicted text is the original text, which is now in conflict with the changed text within the same document. This creates a strong in-text contradiction concerning the duration of obligations, satisfying the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Notice s. Whenever under the terms of or in connection with this Agreement any notice, consent, approval, authorization or other information is proper or required to be given by either party, such notice, consent, approval, authorization or other information shall be in writing and shall be given or made by facsimile, by reputable overnight courier with documentation of receipt to the intended recipient th ereof or by registered or certified mail, return receipt re- quested, and with all postage prepaid, addressed as follows: If to Company, to: LiION LLC 120 Prairie Lake Rd East Dundee, IL 60118 Attention: Gary Gray If to Vendor , to: ____________________________________ Attention:
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Notices. Whenever under the terms of or in connection with this Agreement any notice, consent, approval, authorization or other information is proper or required to be given by either party, such notice, consent, approval, authorization or other information shall be in writing and shall be given or made by facsimile, or verbally. If to Company, to: LiION LLC 120 Prairie Lake Rd East Dundee, IL 60118 Attention: Gary Gray If to Vendor , to: ____________________________________ Attention:
perturbation.explanation: The original specifies that all notices must be in writing. The modified version allows verbal communication in addition to facsimile, creating a direct contradiction regarding the required form of communication for notices, consent, approval, and authorization which creates a contradiction in section 6 itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Notice s. Whenever under the terms of or in connection with this Agreement any notice, consent, approval, authorization or other information is proper or required to be given by either party, such notice, consent, approval, authorization or other information shall be in writing and shall be given or made by facsimile, by reputable overnight courier with documentation of receipt to the intended recipient th ereof or by registered or certified mail, return receipt re- quested, and with all postage prepaid, addressed as follows: If to Company, to: LiION LLC 120 Prairie Lake Rd East Dundee, IL 60118 Attention: Gary Gray If to Vendor , to: ____________________________________ Attention: Either party may change the person to whom notices shall be sent or its notice address by notice given in accordance with this section . Notice shall be deemed received o n the date of receipt or
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that all notices, consents, approvals, authorizations, or other information must be in writing. The changed text modifies this by allowing verbal communication in addition to facsimile. This creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction within section 6, which is also the contradicted text, because the modified version explicitly permits a form of communication (verbal) that was previously excluded, thus conflicting with the original requirement for written communication.

perturbation.original_text: 5. No License or Other Rights . No license or other rights under any patent, copy- right, trademark or trade secret are granted or implied by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. No License or Other Rights. No license or other rights under any patent, copy- right, trademark or trade secret are granted or implied by this Agreement, except for a limited license to use the Disclosing Party's trademarks for marketing purposes, subject to written approval.
perturbation.explanation: The original says there are no license granted. The additional text says that there is a limited license to use trademarks for marketing with written aproval. This adds a contradiction regarding whether any licenses are granted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. No License or Other Rights . No license or other rights under any patent, copy- right, trademark or trade secret are granted or implied by this Agreement. Neither this Agree- ment nor the disclosure or receipt of Confidential Information shall be construed to create any obligation of a party to enter into any agreement or relationship with the other party or to pur- chase from or provide to the other party any service or product. Neither party shall co mmunicate any information to the other in violation of the proprietary rights of any third party.
perturbation.justification: The original and contradicted text state that "No license or other rights under any patent, copy- right, trademark or trade secret are granted or implied by this Agreement." The changed text adds "except for a limited license to use the Disclosing Party's trademarks for marketing purposes, subject to written approval." This directly contradicts the original statement that no licenses are granted, creating an in-text contradiction.
